rulings.Case Title: JESSICA KOERNER ET AL VS. DENNIS GALLAGHER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Profits And Financial Condition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 11, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS JESSICA KOERNER and DANNY FREEMAN's Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Profits And Financial Condition Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CAROLINE MOIA VS. WILLIAMS-SONOMA INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 11, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT WILLIAMS-SONOMA INC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted with the exception of RFPD No. 18 which the Court finds to be vastly overbroad. Plaintiff is directed to serve supplemental responses, and produce responsive documents, by 5/21/18. The supplemental responses to the RFPDs shall state that "all non-privileged documents in Plaintiff's possession have been produced." No qualifiers to the responses shall be allowed. If any documents are withheld on privilege, Plaintiff must provide Defendants with a log of those documents indicating the author and recipient, date, and reason for withholding them. Sanctions in the amount of $300 awarded to Defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CAROLINE MOIA VS. WILLIAMS-SONOMA INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set One And For Sancations Against Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 11, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT WILLIAMS-SONOMA INC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set One And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. While Plaintiff has provided responses, they are deficient either by improperly incorporating references to another interrogatory response and/or by not answering the interrogatory straightforwardly. Several of the responses make reference to "John or Jane Does." Given that trial is quickly approaching, Plaintiff must identify those individuals or provide a response indicating that she does not know who they are. Further responses shall be provided by 5/21/18. Sanctions in the amount of $300 awarded to Defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: DEPARTMENT OF FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING AN VS. FOREVER 21 RETAIL INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 11, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF DEPARTMENT OF FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DFEH's motion for leave to file the first amended complaint is GRANTED. As the court previously ruled, the DFEH can seek recovery of punitive damages in the employment context and Forever 21 overstates the holding of Dyna-Med, Inc. v. Fair Emp. & Housing Comm. (1987) 43 Cal.3d 1379. In that case, the Supreme Court limited its review specifically to the issue of what types of damages the Fair Employment Housing Commission (i.e., the agency) could impose. The Legislature amended FEHA and disbanded the Commission. Since the court-not an agency-is adjudicating the punitives issue, the DFEH can seek recovery of that remedy. Gov't Code 12965(a) allows the DFEH to bring a claim on behalf of real parties. Gov't Code 12965(c) states that "[a] court may grant as relief in any action filed pursuant to subdivision (a) any relief a court is empowered to grant in a civil action brought pursuant to subdivision (b), in addition to any other relief that, in the judgment of the court, will effectuate the purpose of this part." (See also Commodore Home Systems v. Superior Court (1982) 32 Cal.3d 211, 215 (all forms of relief granted to civil litigants generally (including punitive damages where appropriate) may be awarded by court in civil action).) Allowing the amendment does not prejudice Forever 21 as it already brought a motion for summary adjudication that attacked the substantive merits of the punitive damages claim and the trial date is unchanged. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for DFEH is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 11, 2018, Line 10, Plaintiff Gary Kovacs' Motion For An Order Compelling Third Party The Boston Consulting Group To Produce Documents Pursuant To Properly Served Subpoena And For Sanctions. rulings.Case Title: GEODE INVESTMENTS PRIME, LLC ET AL VS. SEAN SHARMA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 11, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANTS SEAN SHARMA, ULTRATICK, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrers to plaintiffs' first amended complaint are all OVERRULED: 1 & 2 (Breach of Contract): The damages pled are not limited to amounts "due or payable pursuant to" the note; in any event, alter ego liability is adequately pled against defendant Sharma. 3 (Fraud): The claim is pled with sufficient specificity. Defendants repeatedly raise factual disputes and argue conclusions from pleaded facts - both are improper at the pleading stage. 4 (Negligent Misrepresentation): Defendants' arguments focus on only one of the several misrepresentations alleged, some of which go to existing fact. 5 (Corp. Code §25401): Existence of a security is adequately pled; the demurrer again raises factual disputes that are improper at the pleading stage. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
